---
title: "R Tip of the Day: ggiraph"
author: "Mary Phillips"
format:
  revealjs: 
    theme: solarized
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: 
      buttons: true
    preview-links: auto
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
#|include: FALSE
library(ggiraph)
library(tidyverse)
library(nycflights13)
library(patchwork)

flights <- nycflights13::flights 
flights_jan1 <- flights |>
  filter(origin == "EWR" & month == 1 & day == 1 & hour == 13) |>
  mutate(name = str_glue("EWR to {dest}, ",
                         "flight num {flight}"))

dinos <- tribble(
  ~type, ~year, ~perc, ~unique,
  "love", 2024, 13, "love 2024",
  "like", 2024, 31, "like 2024",
  "neither", 2024, 47, "neither 2024",
  "dislike", 2024, 2, "dislike 2024",
  "hate", 2024, 3, "hate 2024",
  "don't know", 2024, 4, "don't know 2024",
  "love", 2026, 17, "love 2026",
  "like", 2026, 30, "like 2026",
  "neither", 2026, 47, "neither 2026",
  "dislike", 2026, 2, "dislike 2026",
  "hate", 2026, 1, "hate 2026",
  "don't know", 2026, 3, "don't know 2026"
)
dinos$type <- fct_inorder(dinos$type)
```

## What is ggiraph?

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
![](https://github.com/philli17/R_Tip_of_the_Day/blob/main/ggiraph_hex.png?raw=true)

- [ggiraph is an extension of the ggplot package that allows you to make interactive graphs ]{style="color:darkorange1;"}
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
- It is designed specifically to work with ggplot, so it works well with RMarkdown, Quarto and Shiny.
:::

## How does ggiraph work?
- Each of the geometries that already exist in ggplot get an interactive version

:::{.fragment .fade-in}
- geom_point() in ggiraph is geom_point_interactive()
- geom_bar() is geom_bar_interactive()
- and so on...
:::

:::{.fragment .fade-in}
- Simple and straightforward if you are familiar with ggplot
:::

## So, what can ggiraph do?

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
```{r}
dinoplot <- dinos |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point_interactive(aes(x = type,
             y = perc,
             color = as.factor(year),
             tooltip = unique,
             data_id = unique),
    size = 3, position = "jitter", hover_nearest = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "How much do you like dinosuars?",
       caption = "Data from YouGov's polls, Dinosaurs
       https://yougov.com/en-us/topics/topic/Dinosaurs",
       color = "Year",
       x = "How much do you Like or Dislike dinosaurs?",
       y = "Percentage of Answers")
girafe(ggobj = dinoplot)
```
:::

## Scatterplot Code {auto-animate=true}
```{.r code-line-numbers="3,4,5,6,7,9"}
dinoplot <- dinos |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point_interactive(aes(x = type,
             y = perc,
             color = as.factor(year),
             tooltip = unique,
             data_id = unique),
             size = 3, 
             position = "jitter", 
             hover_nearest = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "How much do you like dinosuars?",
       caption = "Data from YouGov's polls, Dinosaurs
       https://yougov.com/en-us/topics/topic/Dinosaurs",
       color = "Year",
       x = "How much do you Like or Dislike dinosaurs?",
       y = "Percentage of Answers")
girafe(ggobj = dinoplot)
```

## ggiraph arguments
- There are a few arguments that can be added to the ggplot aesthetics.
- tooltip: displays when the mouse hovers over objects
- dataid: id asscoiated with elements
- onclick: JavaScript function executed on click

## Another Example: NYC Flights

```{r}
scatter <- ggplot(
  data = flights_jan1,
  mapping = aes(
    x = dep_delay, y = arr_delay,
    tooltip = name,
    data_id = name
  )
) +
  geom_point_interactive(
    size = 3, hover_nearest = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Departure and Arrival Delays",
    x = "Departure Delay (minutes)", y = "Arrival Delay (minutes)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

bar <- ggplot(
  data = flights_jan1,
  mapping = aes(
    x = reorder(flight, distance), y = distance,
    tooltip = paste(name, "<br>Dist", distance, "mi"),
    data_id = name
  )
) +
  geom_col_interactive(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Flight Distance",
    x = "Flight Number", y = "Flight Distance in Miles"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

combined_plot <- scatter + bar +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)

interactive_plot <- girafe(ggobj = combined_plot)
interactive_plot
```

---

```{.r code-line-numbers="1,2,8,9,10"}
mutate(name = str_glue("EWR to {dest}, ",
"flight num {flight}"))

bar <- ggplot(
  data = flights_jan1,
  mapping = aes(
    x = reorder(flight, distance), y = distance,
    tooltip = paste(name, "<br>Dist", distance, "mi"),
    data_id = name)) +
  geom_col_interactive(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Flight Distance",
    x = "Flight Number", y = "Flight Distance in Miles") 
```

## How to make the graph interactive
It will not be interactive if you don't use the girafe() function

The code won't fail to run or render, it will just create a regular non-interactive ggplot.

:::{.fragment .fade-in}
girafe(ggobj = ____) 

Just insert the plot you've created into the space. ggobj stands for ggobject. You could also create the plot within the function.
:::

# Combining plots 
If you want to use the interactive features for multiple graphs at once, use the patchwork package before you put the plotting code into girafe()

## Multiple Plots + Interactive

```{.r code-line-numbers="1,17,18,20"}
bar <- ggplot(
  data = flights_jan1,
  mapping = aes(
    x = reorder(flight, distance), y = distance,
    tooltip = paste(name, "<br>Dist", distance, "mi"),
    data_id = name
  )
) +
  geom_col_interactive(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Flight Distance",
    x = "Flight Number", y = "Flight Distance in Miles"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

combined_plot <- scatter + bar +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)

interactive_plot <- girafe(ggobj = combined_plot)
```

## Back to Dinosaurs

```{r}
#|include: FALSE
more_dinos <- tribble(
  ~dino_answer, ~uscitizen, 	~likes_or_loves, ~neither_likes_nor_dislikes,
"Tyrannosaurus Rex",	23.686,	28.634,	18.638,
"Brontosaurus",	6.612,	9.659,	4.329,
"Triceratops",	6.457,	10.828,	2.74,
"Pterodactyl", 5.097,	7.16,	3.569,
"Velociraptor",	4.821,	7.756,	2.402,
"Stegosaurus",	3.709,	4.53,	3.386,
"Brachiosaurus",	1.721,	2.864,	0.814,
"Other",	6.31,	8.479,	4.775,
"I don't have a favorite",	39.112,	18.746,	56.062
)
more_dinos$dino_answer <- fct_inorder(rev(more_dinos$dino_answer))
```

```{r}
p1 <- ggplot(
  data = more_dinos,
  mapping = aes(x = likes_or_loves, y = dino_answer, tooltip = paste(likes_or_loves, "%"), data_id = dino_answer)
) +
  geom_col_interactive(
    onclick = paste0(
      "this.style.fill = this.style.fill === \"red\" ? this.getAttribute(\"original-fill\") : \"red\";",
      "this.classList.toggle(\"highlighted\");",
      "var tooltip = document.getElementById(\"custom-tooltip\");",
      "tooltip.innerHTML = \"A chart by: \" + this.getAttribute(\"data-blah\");",
      "tooltip.style.display = \"block\";",
      "tooltip.style.left = (event.pageX + 10) + \"px\";",
      "tooltip.style.top = (event.pageY + 10) + \"px\";",
      "setTimeout(function() { tooltip.style.display = \"none\"; }, 1500);"
    )
  ) +
  labs(x = "Likes or Loves") +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
  )


p2 <- ggplot(
  data = more_dinos,
  mapping = aes(x = neither_likes_nor_dislikes, y = dino_answer, tooltip = paste(neither_likes_nor_dislikes, "%"), data_id = dino_answer)
) +
  geom_col_interactive(
    onclick = paste0(
      "this.style.fill = this.style.fill === \"red\" ? this.getAttribute(\"original-fill\") : \"red\";",
      "this.classList.toggle(\"highlighted\");",
      "var tooltip = document.getElementById(\"custom-tooltip\");",
      "tooltip.innerHTML = \"A chart by: \" + this.getAttribute(\"data-blah\");",
      "tooltip.style.display = \"block\";",
      "tooltip.style.left = (event.pageX + 10) + \"px\";",
      "tooltip.style.top = (event.pageY + 10) + \"px\";",
      "setTimeout(function() { tooltip.style.display = \"none\"; }, 1500);"
    )
  ) +
  labs(x = "Neither Likes Nor Dislikes") +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

p3 <- ggplot(
  data = more_dinos,
  mapping = aes(x = uscitizen, y = dino_answer, tooltip = paste(uscitizen, "%"), data_id = dino_answer)
) +
  geom_col_interactive(
    onclick = paste0(
      "this.style.fill = this.style.fill === \"red\" ? this.getAttribute(\"original-fill\") : \"red\";",
      "this.classList.toggle(\"highlighted\");",
      "var tooltip = document.getElementById(\"custom-tooltip\");",
      "tooltip.innerHTML = \"A chart by: \" + this.getAttribute(\"data-blah\");",
      "tooltip.style.display = \"block\";",
      "tooltip.style.left = (event.pageX + 10) + \"px\";",
      "tooltip.style.top = (event.pageY + 10) + \"px\";",
      "setTimeout(function() { tooltip.style.display = \"none\"; }, 1500);"
    )
  ) +
  labs(x = "US Adult Citizen") +
theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )


combined_plot <- p1 + p2 + p3 +
  plot_layout(ncol = 3)

interactive_plot <- girafe(ggobj = combined_plot)
interactive_plot
```

## The Sky is the Limit with JavaScript


There are ways to add even more visuals and animations to your graphs by combining ggiraph and JavaScript

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
Including confetti!


```{.r}
onclick = paste0("confetti({
        particleCount: 1000,
        spread: 70,
        origin: { y: 0.6 }
      });"
```
:::

---

![](https://github.com/philli17/R_Tip_of_the_Day/blob/main/Confetti-ezgif.com-optimize.gif?raw=true){fig-align="center" width="23%"}

## Sources
[here](https://davidgohel.github.io/ggiraph/) is the guide to the package on github

https://r-graph-gallery.com/package/ggiraph.html

https://albert-rapp.de/posts/ggplot2-tips/17_ggiraph/17_ggiraph.html
